An ancient riverbank revived as a canal when the Amu Darya shifted westwards in the 9th century. Mangit and On Tort Uriw Qaraqalpaqs settled along the canal as refugees and captives during the 17-18th centuries, and used it as an artery for trade. Mainly used for irrigation of wheat and sorghum fields.
Named after a Qaraqalpaq Quwanish. An extension of the Kok Uzak.
A floodplain, where Qaraqalpaqs conducted semi-nomadic livelihoods (upstream in the summer, dowsntream in the winter). Mixed use: grazing, farming, fishing and foraging.
